headlines.headline: G7 leaders discuss granting trusted partners access to cutting-edge US AI models
headlines.summary: G7 leaders discussed a plan to grant select trusted partners access to advanced AI models from US firms such as Anthropic, three diplomatic sources said, potentially opening a path around restrictions on non-American use of the technology.

headlines.headline: Israeli drone strikes kill at least 4 in southern Lebanon
headlines.summary: Israeli drone strikes targeted three vehicles in southern Lebanon, killing at least four people and wounding others, Lebanons National News Agency reported. The strikes come amid ongoing tensions even as the US-Iran peace deal raises hopes for regional de-escalation.

headlines.headline: Trump-backed candidate loses Georgia governor runoff in upset
headlines.summary: Georgia Republican voters rejected President Trumps preferred nominee for the competitive open governor race, delivering a rare primary endorsement defeat. Rep. Mike Collins won the Republican Senate runoff and will face Sen. Jon Ossoff in a closely watched matchup.

headlines.headline: Trump signals swift return of sanctions on Russian oil as G7 refocuses on Ukraine
headlines.summary: The US could soon reimpose sanctions on Russian oil shipments after President Trump and fellow G7 leaders moved to refocus on Ukraine, with the Iran peace deal creating conditions for tighter enforcement. Canada also announced new sanctions targeting Russias shadow fleet and energy revenues.

headlines.headline: White House talking points on Iran deal dont match reality, AP analysis finds
headlines.summary: The White House proclaimed major victories in its Iran talking points, including Iran agreeing to never have a nuclear weapon and reopening the Strait of Hormuz. But the AP found several claims go against realities on the ground, especially regarding what Israel has agreed to in Lebanon. The MOU remains a closely guarded secret even among Republican allies.

headlines.headline: Lebanon prepares new round of talks with Israel as president and PM coordinate strategy
headlines.summary: Lebanese leaders discussed preparations for a new round of negotiations with Israel next week, as the US-Iran deal raises prospects for broader regional de-escalation and Israel-Hezbollah tensions ease.

headlines.headline: Trump to host Iraqi Prime Minister at White House in mid-July
headlines.summary: US President Trump will host Iraqi PM Ali al-Zaidi at the White House next month, as Washington reshapes Middle East alliances following the Iran peace deal.

headlines.headline: Canada Carney raises China EV tariffs with Trump at G7, announces new Russia sanctions
headlines.summary: Canadian PM Mark Carney held a short but intense discussion with Trump on China EV trade at the G7 summit, while also announcing fresh sanctions against Russia over the Ukraine war.

headlines.headline: US holds off blacklisting China's DeepSeek and 100+ flagged firms to avoid escalating Beijing tensions
headlines.summary: The Trump administration has delayed adding DeepSeek, CXMT, and over 100 Chinese companies flagged as national security risks to the trade blacklist, seeking to avoid further escalation with Beijing amid fragile diplomatic ties.

headlines.headline: European leaders test Iran deal risks at G7, urge rethink on Ukraine strategy
headlines.summary: European allies at the G7 summit raised concerns about the US-Iran interim deal's implications while pushing Trump to reconsider his approach to Ukraine, as the summit revealed persistent transatlantic divisions.

perspectives.angle: European leaders warn Trump on Iran deal risks, push Ukraine rethink at G7 summit
perspectives.summary: European leaders told Trump at the G7 summit in Evian that a superficial interim Iran deal risks entrenching Tehran's nuclear and missile programs, while pressing him to rethink his Ukraine strategy and stand behind Kyiv.

headlines.headline: Trump says Ukraine war has no impact on US as G7 reveals persistent ally divisions
headlines.summary: President Trump downplayed the Ukraine war's significance for the US at the G7 summit, even as allied leaders pushed for stronger collective action and the mood around the Iran deal lightened the overall agenda.

perspectives.angle: Trump tells G7 Ukraine war has "no impact" on the United States
perspectives.summary: At the G7 summit in Evian, President Trump distanced the US from the Ukraine conflict, telling allies the war has no effect on America — a stark signal to European partners who have relied on US security guarantees for decades.

headlines.headline: Trump vows to release text of Iran agreement within days as key questions remain unanswered
headlines.summary: At the G7 summit, Trump promised to publish the full text of the US-Iran interim deal soon, but critical details about enforcement, timelines, and Israel's Lebanon withdrawal demands remain unresolved.

headlines.headline: Oil prices fall 5% to 3-month low on Strait of Hormuz reopening hopes
headlines.summary: Crude prices dropped sharply for a second straight session as details of the US-Iran interim deal emerged, including an agreement to allow Iran to sell oil and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

perspectives.angle: World largest tanker operator says Hormuz oil flow may take weeks or a month to resume
perspectives.summary: The CEO of Japan Mitsui OSK Lines, the worlds largest tanker operator, cautioned that many operators will wait before resuming Strait of Hormuz transits even after a deal, delaying full oil flow recovery.

headlines.headline: Zelenskiy says G7 agreed Russia is not winning, discussed more sanctions
headlines.summary: Ukrainian President Zelenskiy said G7 leaders agreed that Russia is not winning the war in Ukraine and discussed additional sanctions to bring Moscow to the negotiating table.

perspectives.angle: Zelenskiy says G7 leaders agreed Russia is not winning, discussed further sanctions
perspectives.summary: Ukraine's president said G7 leaders reached consensus that Russia is not winning the war and discussed additional sanctions to push Moscow toward negotiations, after a closed-door session with Trump at the Evian summit.

headlines.headline: Gold holds gains as US and Iran prepare to sign interim peace deal
headlines.summary: Gold prices held steady as markets assessed the implications of the US-Iran interim peace deal, which could ease global inflationary pressures and reshape energy markets.

headlines.headline: British couple recount Russian warship firing warning shots near their boat
headlines.summary: A retired British couple described warning shots fired near their vessel by a Russian warship in international waters, highlighting ongoing maritime tensions between Russia and Western civilians.

headlines.headline: Australia relaxes Middle East travel warnings after US-Iran interim deal
headlines.summary: Australia lowered travel advisories for Bahrain, Israel, Kuwait, Qatar, and the UAE following the US-Iran interim peace agreement, though it warned the security situation could still deteriorate rapidly with little warning.

headlines.headline: US-Iran deal permits oil sales, includes access to  billion private fund
headlines.summary: Details of the US-Iran interim agreement reveal Tehran will be allowed to sell oil upon signing and gain access to a  billion private fund. Trump also intensified criticism of Netanyahu over the Lebanon campaign.

headlines.headline: Latin America and Europe are bystanders in the US-China AI race, says Lula adviser
headlines.summary: Brazilian president Lula's top adviser Celso Amorim told a Rio conference that the Global South must respond through BRICS, framing rare earths and data sovereignty as strategic priorities in the emerging AI contest.

headlines.headline: The Iran War Permanently Altered the Global Economy
headlines.summary: The New York Times analyzes how the US-Iran war has fundamentally reshaped global trade patterns and oil markets, arguing that even with a peace deal, the economic order is unlikely to revert to its pre-war state.

perspectives.angle: The Iran war permanently altered the global economy
perspectives.summary: The New York Times analyzes how the US-Iran conflict has fundamentally reshaped global trade, energy markets, and economic alliances in ways unlikely to revert even with a peace deal.

headlines.headline: Lebanon Suffers Deadliest Spillover of the Regional War as Israel-Hezbollah Conflict Takes Heavy Toll
headlines.summary: A Reuters factbox details the devastating costs to Lebanon from the latest Israel-Hezbollah war — the deadliest spillover of the regional conflict triggered by US-Israeli strikes on Iran more than three months ago.

headlines.headline: Iran War — Energy Shock or Geopolitical Turning Point?
headlines.summary: Reuters commentary examines whether the Iran war will be remembered as a temporary energy shock or a lasting watershed moment for global crude oil and LNG markets, as a US-Iran peace deal promises to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.headline: G7 Leaders Meet Zelenskyy, Qatar and UAE Heads on Summit First Full Day
headlines.summary: NPR reports that President Trump met with G7 leaders as well as Ukrainian President Zelenskyy and the heads of Qatar and UAE during the key summit first full day, as allies push Ukraine back onto the agenda.

headlines.headline: Trump denies US will invest money in Iran, says $300B fund report is "ridiculous"
headlines.summary: At the G7 summit, President Trump told reporters the US is "not investing any money in Iran" and dismissed reports of a $300 billion investment fund. VP Vance said any fund would come from the Gulf Coalition, not US taxpayers.

perspectives.angle: Trump dismisses reports of $300B Iran reconstruction fund as fake news
perspectives.summary: President Trump pushed back on reports that a $300 billion reconstruction fund for Iran would be part of the peace agreement, casting doubt on one of the most controversial rumored elements of the deal.

headlines.headline: G7 allies push Ukraine back onto agenda as Zelenskyy joins leaders for talks
headlines.summary: Ukraine's allies at the G7 summit scrambled to refocus attention on the war with Russia, which had been overshadowed by the Iran conflict. Zelenskyy joined leaders for a 75-minute working session. The UK announced new sanctions on Russia's shadow fleet and seized a Russian vessel in the English Channel.

headlines.headline: UK announces new sanctions on Russian shadow fleet, seizes vessel in English Channel
headlines.summary: The UK unveiled a new package of sanctions targeting Russia's shadow oil fleet and finance networks. British troops seized a Russian shadow fleet vessel in the English Channel for the first time. The move comes as G7 leaders push to squeeze Russian oil revenues.

headlines.headline: US diplomats face fallout over Islamic State prisoner transfers from Syria
headlines.summary: American diplomats are aware of fresh allegations regarding Islamic State terrorism detainees transferred from Syria, raising concerns about security and accountability as the US reshapes its Middle East posture.

headlines.headline: China cruising to become world top tourism market with visa waivers and high-speed rail
headlines.summary: Visa waivers for over 70 countries and surging domestic travel are pushing China toward overtaking the US as the worlds largest tourism market, reshaping global tourism patterns.

headlines.headline: Hong Kong Science Park partners with SenseTime to build home-grown AI data center
headlines.summary: Hong Kong Science Park and SenseTime are partnering to build a domestic AI data center targeting 40,000 petaflops by 2030, amid growing competition in the regional AI infrastructure race.

headlines.headline: Trump says Iran deal to be public soon, will rule out nuclear weapon for Tehran
headlines.summary: Details of the US-Iran interim deal emerged at the G7 summit, with Trump saying the accord will be made public soon and will prohibit Iran from developing a nuclear weapon. How the deal will work in practice remains unclear.

perspectives.angle: Trump says he will send Iran deal to Congress for approval
perspectives.summary: President Trump told reporters at the G7 summit he would send the Iran peace agreement to Congress for approval, saying "I like the idea." Sen. Lindsey Graham expressed concern that Iran's view of the deal seems different from what the US negotiating team claims.

headlines.headline: Canada imposes sanctions on Russias shadow fleet and energy revenues at G7
headlines.summary: PM Carney announced new sanctions targeting Russias shadow fleet, energy revenues, defense-industrial sector, and disinformation entities at the G7 summit, as allies push to refocus pressure on Moscow over Ukraine.

headlines.headline: White House Iran deal talking points often dont meet reality, AP finds
headlines.summary: An AP analysis finds that the White Houses claims of accomplishments in the Iran war deal frequently diverge from the actual terms and realities of the agreement.

headlines.headline: Deal or no deal, oil prices will stay high for months
headlines.summary: Even with the US-Iran peace accord, The Economist reports that global oil prices are likely to remain elevated for months as physical supply chains and shipping routes take time to normalize.

headlines.headline: Bank of Japan raises rates to 31-year high, signals further hikes ahead
headlines.summary: The Bank of Japan raised interest rates to their highest level in 31 years in a landmark policy normalization step, signaling readiness to tighten further as it focuses on taming price pressures from the Iran-war-induced energy shock.

perspectives.angle: Bank of Japan raises key interest rate to 1%, highest in three decades
perspectives.summary: The Bank of Japan raised its benchmark rate to 1%, a 31-year high, citing inflation pressures from the Middle East conflict and a weak yen. The Nikkei 225 briefly topped 70,000 for the first time before retreating.

headlines.headline: Trump meets world leaders at G7 as Iran deal takes center stage
headlines.summary: World leaders sought clarity on the Iran deal framework at the G7 summit, with Iran demanding Israel withdraw from Lebanon as part of the agreement, while Trump said Netanyahu needs to be "more responsible."

perspectives.angle: G7 Allies Seek Clarity on Iran Deal as Trump Arrives in France
perspectives.summary: World leaders gathering at the G7 summit in France pressed for details on the US-Iran memorandum of understanding, with questions remaining about enforcement, the Strait of Hormuz reopening timeline, and Israel-Hezbollah tensions that could threaten the deal.

headlines.headline: Israel Refuses Lebanon Withdrawal Despite Iran Deal Pressure
headlines.summary: Israel says it will not withdraw forces from Lebanon even as Iran insists the US-Iran peace deal requires Israeli troops to leave. Hezbollah claims Iran has pledged to pursue Israeli withdrawal as a condition for any final nuclear agreement.

perspectives.angle: Israel Lebanon stance threatens to unravel US-Iran agreement
perspectives.summary: The US-Iran ceasefire deal hinges on Israel accepting a ceasefire in Lebanon, something it is reluctant to do, creating a key obstacle as mediators try to finalize terms this week.

headlines.headline: US Military Plans Permanent Weapons Stockpile in Australia
headlines.summary: US Navy documents reveal advanced planning for a large war-ready weapons stockpile in Australia, with $30 million allocated to build warehouses and offices. The move signals deeper US military posture in the Indo-Pacific.

headlines.headline: Ukrainian Su-24 Bomber Crashes, Both Pilots Killed
headlines.summary: A Ukrainian Su-24 bomber crashed in Khmelnytskyi Oblast on the evening of June 16, killing both the pilot and navigator. The Air Force confirmed the incident, with the cause under investigation.

headlines.headline: Trump signals swift return of sanctions on Russian oil as G7 refocuses on Ukraine
headlines.summary: At the G7 summit in France, Trump indicated he may reimpose sanctions on Russian oil soon, as world leaders pushed Ukraine back to the top of the agenda alongside the Iran deal.

perspectives.angle: Trump signals swift return of sanctions on Russian oil as G7 refocuses on Ukraine
perspectives.summary: President Trump and fellow G7 leaders moved to reimpose sanctions on Russian oil shipments, shifting focus back to the Ukraine war after the Iran deal dominated early summit discussions.

perspectives.angle: G7 allies push Trump to reinstate sanctions on Russian oil as summit refocuses on Ukraine
perspectives.summary: U.S. allies at the G7 summit pressed President Trump to swiftly reimpose sanctions on Russian oil, shifting focus back to Ukraine amid signs the Iran war is de-escalating. The move signals continued Western resolve on isolating Moscow economically.

headlines.headline: Ukrainian drone strike halts operations at Moscow oil refinery
headlines.summary: A Ukrainian drone attack sparked a fire at Moscows largest fuel-supplying refinery, halting operations. Zelenskiy described the strike as coercion to push Russia toward peace talks.

perspectives.angle: Ukrainian drone strike halts operations at Moscow oil refinery
perspectives.summary: A Ukrainian drone attack sparked a fire and halted operations at the largest fuel supplier to the Moscow region, marking a significant escalation in Ukrainian strikes deep inside Russian territory amid the G7 summit.

headlines.headline: Trump insists Strait of Hormuz will open toll-free under Iran deal
headlines.summary: Speaking at the G7 summit, Trump declared the Strait of Hormuz would reopen toll-free under the US-Iran deal, as oil prices fell 5% to a three-month low on hopes of restored shipping.

perspectives.angle: Strait of Hormuz sees mini exodus of shipping as Vance insists peace deal benefits US
perspectives.summary: Ships are rushing to leave the Strait of Hormuz amid uncertainty over the US-Iran peace deal terms, while Vice President JD Vance defended the agreement as advantageous for American interests.

headlines.headline: ECB will stay proactive on inflation even after Iran deal, Lane says
headlines.summary: ECB chief economist Philip Lane said the central bank will remain proactive in fighting high inflation despite falling energy prices following the US-Iran deal, signaling rates may stay elevated.

perspectives.angle: ECB chief Lagarde welcomes Iran ceasefire but warns inflation may persist
perspectives.summary: ECB President Christine Lagarde called the US-Iran ceasefire good news that could help reopen the Strait of Hormuz, but several ECB colleagues cautioned it would not immediately bring down elevated euro zone inflation.

headlines.headline: Iran soccer team forced to train in Tijuana after being blocked from U.S. entry
headlines.summary: Iran World Cup squad was denied entry to train in the United States amid wartime tensions, finding an unlikely welcome in the Mexican border city of Tijuana — a story where sport and geopolitics collide.

headlines.headline: Anti-capitalist protesters clash with security at G7 summit in Evian
headlines.summary: Thousands of anti-capitalist demonstrators protested near the G7 summit venue in Evian-les-Bains, opposing what they called the elite global order — a familiar backdrop for the annual gathering of world leaders.

headlines.headline: AI price war deepens across China as competition intensifies between tech giants
headlines.summary: Narrowing capability gaps between AI models and falling service costs are fueling a price war among Chinese tech firms, with analysts predicting further reductions as competition heats up.

headlines.headline: Harris says Trump DOJ investigation of Newsom fits pattern of targeting political enemies
headlines.summary: Former Vice President Kamala Harris condemned the Trump administration investigation into California Governor Gavin Newsom, calling it part of a broader pattern of using the Justice Department against political opponents.

headlines.headline: Swiss luxury resort chosen as venue for US-Iran deal signing ceremony
headlines.summary: The Bürgenstock resort above Lake Lucerne in Switzerland, long favored by world leaders and celebrities, will host the formal signing of the US-Iran peace agreement scheduled for June 19.

perspectives.angle: Trump says next phase of Iran deal will be "easier" as Geneva signing set for June 19
perspectives.summary: Speaking at the G7 summit, Trump expressed confidence that the Iran peace deal's next phase would proceed smoothly, with a formal signing ceremony planned in Geneva on June 19 after the framework MOU was electronically signed.

headlines.headline: Philippines unprepared for a mega-quake despite Mindanao disaster, experts warn
headlines.summary: A 50-year-old building code and stalled political action leave the Philippines vulnerable to a catastrophic earthquake, with scientists warning the recent Mindanao quake is only a preview of a larger event.

headlines.headline: Trump rebukes Israel at G7, suggests Syria should handle Hezbollah instead
headlines.summary: President Trump publicly criticized Israel's prolonged and deadly campaign in Lebanon, saying the war is "too long" and suggesting Damascus — not the IDF — should deal with Hezbollah, a proposal that could reshape the Levant's security landscape.

headlines.headline: Economist briefing: Trump details Iran nuclear terms, says Iran will never build a weapon
headlines.summary: The Economist reports Trump's MOU with Iran guarantees Tehran will never develop a nuclear weapon, though key implementation details remain unclear. The deal's impact on oil markets and regional security is still unfolding.

headlines.headline: Russian Warship Fires Warning Shots at Yacht in the English Channel
headlines.summary: A Russian frigate fired warning shots at a yacht that came near it while transiting the English Channel, escalating tensions in European waters just days after the UK seized a Russian-linked tanker.

perspectives.angle: Russian warship fires warning shots at UK-registered yacht in the English Channel
perspectives.summary: A Russian warship fired warning shots near a British pleasure yacht about 20 nautical miles south of the Isle of Wight, just outside UK territorial waters. The UK Ministry of Defence is investigating the incident amid heightened tensions between London and Moscow.

headlines.headline: US Using Iranian Smuggling Tactics to Sneak Oil Out of the Gulf
headlines.summary: Reuters reports the United States has adopted Iranian-style ship-to-ship transfer tactics to bypass the Hormuz blockade and move crude oil out of the Persian Gulf during the conflict, highlighting how the standoff disrupted global energy logistics.

perspectives.angle: US Military Adopts Iranian Smuggling Tactic to Move Oil Through Hormuz
perspectives.summary: The US military has been running covert ship-to-ship oil transfers near the Strait of Hormuz since May, using aerial and water drones plus helicopters to guide convoys to awaiting tankers — adopting tactics Iran pioneered for sanctions evasion.

headlines.headline: B-52 Bomber Crashes at Edwards Air Force Base, All 8 Crew Killed
headlines.summary: A US Air Force B-52 Stratofortress crashed during a test flight at Edwards AFB in California, killing all eight aboard including two Boeing employees. The aircraft burst into flames after takeoff during a radar modernization test mission.

perspectives.angle: US Air Force B-52 bomber crashes at Edwards AFB, all 8 crew killed
perspectives.summary: A B-52 Stratofortress crashed shortly after takeoff at Edwards Air Force Base in California, bursting into flames and killing all eight crew members aboard, including two Boeing employees.

headlines.headline: War-weary Russia is fracturing as domestic dissent grows, analysis warns
headlines.summary: An opinion analysis in The Hill argues that mounting internal pressures — from war fatigue to economic strain — are pushing Russia toward a breaking point, with unpredictable consequences for the Ukraine conflict.

headlines.headline: Zelensky seeks US license to produce anti-ballistic missiles domestically
headlines.summary: President Zelensky said Ukraine is requesting US approval to manufacture anti-ballistic missile systems on Ukrainian soil, a significant shift that would bolster air defense capabilities against Russian strikes.

headlines.headline: WWII Death Railway station built by Allied POWs resurfaces in Thailand after 40 years
headlines.summary: A long-lost railway station along Thailand's infamous Death Railway, constructed by Allied prisoners of war under Japanese captivity, has re-emerged after four decades underwater due to changing water levels.

headlines.headline: Lukashenko Says Russia and Ukraine Must Compromise to End War
headlines.summary: Belarusian President Alexander Lukashenko, one of Putin's closest allies, said both sides in the Russia-Ukraine war must compromise, calling the conflict now in its fifth year with no end in sight.

perspectives.angle: Lukashenko urges both sides to compromise to end Russia-Ukraine war
perspectives.summary: Belarusian President Lukashenko, one of Putin's closest allies, publicly called for compromise from both Russia and Ukraine to end the war, a notable shift in rhetoric from a key Kremlin ally.

headlines.headline: Iran World Cup team ordered out of US after opening match
headlines.summary: Iran's coach says the team was ordered to leave the United States and return to its training base in Mexico after a 2-2 draw with New Zealand, highlighting how geopolitical tensions are spilling into the World Cup.

headlines.headline: Iran says final nuclear deal talks with US to begin this week
headlines.summary: Iran announced that negotiations on a comprehensive nuclear agreement and sanctions relief with the United States would likely begin later this week, following the memorandum of understanding signed to end the war.

perspectives.angle: JD Vance admits unresolved issues remain in Iran peace deal ahead of G7 talks
perspectives.summary: Vice President JD Vance acknowledged that several issues still remain in the US-Iran peace agreement as G7 leaders prepare to discuss the Middle East, tempering expectations for a finalized deal at the summit.

headlines.headline: Energy firms brace for new era despite Hormuz reopening deal
headlines.summary: Oil and gas majors expect a quick reopening of the Strait of Hormuz but warn that a full return to normal for the Gulf energy industry is unlikely after more than three months of disrupted shipping.

headlines.headline: Canada announces new Russia sanctions after Carney-Zelensky G7 meeting
headlines.summary: Canada unveiled a new sanctions package targeting 162 individuals, entities, and vessels linked to Russia’s shadow fleet and energy sector following a meeting between PM Carney and Zelensky at the G7 summit.

headlines.headline: Ukraine to create new drone units to bolster northern border defenses
headlines.summary: Commander-in-chief Syrskyi announced Ukraine will form new drone units to strengthen defenses along the northern border with Russia and Belarus amid ongoing security concerns.

headlines.headline: FBI disrupts planned attacks targeting White House UFC event, multiple arrests made
headlines.summary: FBI Director Kash Patel announced that law enforcement disrupted planned attacks targeting the UFC Freedom 250 event at the White House, with at least five people arrested in connection with an alleged drone-explosive plot.

perspectives.angle: FBI foils drone and sniper plot targeting White House UFC event, five arrested
perspectives.summary: The FBI arrested five people in connection with an explosive drone and sniper attack planned against the UFC Freedom 250 event on the White House South Lawn, in what officials described as a major security breach.

headlines.headline: US-Iran deal promises end to war but implementation remains unclear
headlines.summary: Doubts swirled around the US-Iran agreement as questions persist about enforcement mechanisms, with shipping traffic and energy exports expected to take weeks to recover through the Strait of Hormuz.

perspectives.angle: US and Iran sign MOU to end war, formal signing ceremony set for Friday
perspectives.summary: Senior US officials confirmed the memorandum of understanding has been signed electronically, with a formal ceremony planned for Friday. Shipping traffic through the Strait of Hormuz will gradually ramp up as mines are cleared.

headlines.headline: Trump hints at renewed Ukraine focus, says Iran moving to rear-view mirror
headlines.summary: After a very good meeting with Zelenskyy at the G7, Trump said Russia should make a deal and suggested Ukraine was moving back up his priority list as the Iran conflict recedes.

headlines.headline: France and Britain push multinational Hormuz naval mission as ceasefire nears
headlines.summary: France and Britain are advancing plans for a coalition naval mission to safeguard Strait of Hormuz shipping if the US-Iran ceasefire holds, though Irans willingness to accept such an operation remains uncertain.

perspectives.angle: France-UK Coalition Ready to Deploy Hormuz Security Mission After Ceasefire
perspectives.summary: A Franco-British naval coalition of roughly 20 countries is prepared to deploy to the Strait of Hormuz to restart commercial shipping. France has committed its nuclear-powered carrier Charles de Gaulle as the flagship amid expectations that traffic will resume Friday under the peace deal.

headlines.headline: Macron and Zelenskyy caught on camera game-planning how to handle Trump at G7
headlines.summary: Footage captured the French president and Ukrainian leader strategizing together before meeting Trump at the G7 summit, highlighting European concerns about Trumps shifting priorities after the Iran deal.

perspectives.angle: Macron and Zelenskyy caught on camera game-planning how to handle Trump at G7
perspectives.summary: Footage captured the French president and Ukrainian leader strategizing together before meeting Trump at the G7 summit, highlighting European concerns about Trumps shifting priorities after the Iran deal.

headlines.headline: Oil spot premiums fall to pre-war levels after US-Iran deal, shipping caution remains
headlines.summary: Asian crude spot premiums have dropped back to levels seen before the US-Iran war following the tentative ceasefire deal, though uncertainty about how quickly normal Hormuz shipping can resume is keeping a floor under prices.

perspectives.angle: Oil spot premiums fall to pre-war levels after US-Iran deal, shipping caution remains
perspectives.summary: Asian crude spot premiums have dropped back to levels seen before the US-Iran war following the tentative ceasefire deal, though uncertainty about how quickly normal Hormuz shipping can resume is keeping a floor under prices.

perspectives.angle: Oil rebounds on concerns about Iran deal details and Hormuz reopening delay
perspectives.summary: Oil prices rebounded on concerns that the preliminary US-Iran peace agreement lacks detail and that resuming supply through the Strait of Hormuz could take longer than expected due to mine-clearance operations.

headlines.headline: Hormuz mine-clearing could delay full shipping return by 40–50 days
headlines.summary: Demining operations involving conventional minesweepers and underwater drones may take 40–50 days before insurers and shipping companies feel confident enough to resume normal traffic through the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.headline: France to try 14 over Channel disaster that left 27 Iraqi Kurdish migrants dead
headlines.summary: Fourteen people face trial in France over the deaths of at least 27 migrants in the November 2021 English Channel sinking — the deadliest recorded crossing disaster — in a case that could reshape people-smuggling prosecution across Europe.

headlines.headline: Red Cross warns Congo Ebola epidemic yet to peak, may last a year
headlines.summary: A Red Cross official warned that the Ebola epidemic in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo has not yet peaked and could persist for a year, deepening the humanitarian crisis in the conflict-ridden region.

headlines.headline: Trump warns Netanyahu to be 'more responsible' as US-Israel rift deepens over Iran deal
headlines.summary: President Trump publicly rebuked Netanyahu, saying Israel must act more responsibly regarding Lebanon and Hezbollah, as the US-Israel rift over the Iran agreement widens and Iran demands Israeli withdrawal from Lebanon.

perspectives.angle: Congress reacts with skepticism to Trump-Iran deal, demands briefing on secret terms
perspectives.summary: Republican and Democratic senators alike expressed deep skepticism about the US-Iran agreement, with GOP hawks calling it a surrender and Democrats demanding immediate classified briefings on the deal’s undisclosed terms.

headlines.headline: New Fed Chair Warsh faces hawkish shift as first meeting looms
headlines.summary: Kevin Warsh convenes his first Federal Reserve meeting with rate hike expectations rising and the dot plot expected to show a hawkish shift, just weeks after replacing Jerome Powell.

headlines.headline: Japan PM Takaichi pushes coordinated G7 response to China trade tactics
headlines.summary: At the G7 summit, Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi urged allies to develop a united front against Beijing's use of export controls and trade leverage, signaling rising concern over China Shock 2.0.

headlines.headline: Zelensky, Trump, Macron Hold Behind-the-Scenes Meeting at G7 Summit
headlines.summary: Ukrainian President Zelensky met face-to-face with Trump and Macron on the sidelines of the G7 summit in Evian, their first encounter in nearly four months, as Kyiv pushes to revive peace negotiations.

headlines.headline: US Military Plans Permanent War-Ready Weapons Stockpile in Australia
headlines.summary: The US military is planning a permanent war-ready weapons stockpile for its Marine Corps on Australias southeast coast, positioned beyond the range of most Chinese missiles, according to tender documents and officials.

headlines.headline: Japan Undecided on Sending Military Forces to Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: Japan said Tuesday it has not yet decided whether its military will join international efforts to help resume shipping through the Strait of Hormuz following the US-Iran ceasefire agreement.

perspectives.angle: Macron Tells Trump European Forces Ready to Deploy in Strait of Hormuz
perspectives.summary: At the G7 summit opening in Evian-les-Bains, French President Macron told Trump that European naval forces are prepared to help secure the Strait of Hormuz once the US-Iran deal is signed.

headlines.headline: Lukashenko Says Belarus Poses No Military Threat to Ukraine, Apologizes to Zelensky
headlines.summary: Belarusian leader Alexander Lukashenko stated Belarus poses no military threat to Ukraine and apologized to President Zelensky for previous harsh remarks, a notable diplomatic signal as G7 leaders discuss pathways to ending the Russia-Ukraine war.

headlines.headline: Iran soccer team blocked from training in US, welcomed by Tijuana amid World Cup
headlines.summary: Iran's World Cup squad was denied entry to train in the United States amid the recent conflict, but the Mexican border city of Tijuana rolled out a welcome for the team caught between sport and geopolitics.

headlines.headline: Netanyahu and Trump on Collision Course as US-Iran Deal Falls Short of Israeli War Goals
headlines.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu pushed back against the US-Iran peace agreement, declaring that "the struggle has not ended." The deal falls short of Israel's stated war objectives, creating a rift between the two allies at a critical moment.

perspectives.angle: Trump lashes out at Netanyahu, says Israel hindering Iran peace efforts
perspectives.summary: Donald Trump unleashed a new tirade against Benjamin Netanyahu, claiming Israel is obstructing peace efforts with Iran and repeating that there would be no Israel without him, testing their political alliance.

perspectives.angle: Trump criticizes Netanyahu over Lebanon attacks at G7, warns Israel to be more responsible
perspectives.summary: At the G7 summit, Trump publicly criticized Israeli PM Netanyahu for escalating attacks on Lebanon and Hezbollah, saying Israel must act more responsibly — a rare rift between the two leaders on full display before other world leaders.

headlines.headline: Historic Kyiv Pechersk Lavra Monastery Set Ablaze in Russian Strike
headlines.summary: A nearly 1,000-year-old monastery — one of Ukraine's most sacred cultural sites with golden domes towering over Kyiv for centuries — was set ablaze during a Russian attack. Firefighters worked to contain the damage to the historic cathedral famed for its frescoes and silver artefacts.

perspectives.angle: Russian strike damages historic Kyiv cathedral, kills 11 across Ukraine
perspectives.summary: A major Russian overnight barrage of missiles and drones killed at least 11 people across Ukraine and set fire to the nearly 1,000-year-old Kyiv Pechersk Lavra, one of the country's most sacred cultural landmarks. Ukraine's Orthodox leader called Putin "Antichrist" for the attack.

headlines.headline: Ukraine drives rush for mini drone laser-targeting systems as battlefield demand surges
headlines.summary: Defense companies are racing to miniaturize laser target-marking technology for small UAVs after Ukraine requested compact systems suited to its drone-heavy battlefield tactics, spurring new product launches across the industry.

headlines.headline: China develops military stealth coating that could be mass-produced as cheap house paint
headlines.summary: Chinese researchers have created a new microwave-absorbing material that could make stealth technology far cheaper to produce, sparking debate over reports that commercial stealth paint may already be available on the open market.

headlines.headline: Central banks accelerate gold buying as de-dollarization trend deepens worldwide
headlines.summary: The World Gold Council reports that nearly 90% of central banks plan to increase gold holdings as countries diversify reserves away from the US dollar, reflecting a structural shift in global finance amid geopolitical fragmentation.

headlines.headline: Surging Chinese exports threaten European economy, raising alarm at G7 summit
headlines.summary: Eight years of US tariffs have failed to dent China industrial output, and a new wave of cheap exports is now pressuring European manufacturers, forcing G7 leaders to confront an escalating trade crisis.

headlines.headline: Norway Crown Princess son convicted of rape, sentenced to four years
headlines.summary: Marius Borg Høiby, eldest son of Norway Crown Princess Mette-Marit, was found guilty of two counts of rape and sentenced to four years in prison in a case that has shaken the Norwegian royal family.

headlines.headline: Takaichi pushes mineral stockpiling plan at G7, seeks to bridge divides as Japan’s Iron Lady takes the stage
headlines.summary: Japanese PM Sanae Takaichi proposed a joint critical minerals stockpiling initiative at the G7 summit to counter China’s dominance, while also welcoming the US-Iran memorandum as a step toward regional de-escalation.

perspectives.angle: Japan PM Takaichi proposes G7 framework for stockpiling critical minerals
perspectives.summary: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi pitched a plan at the G7 summit for coordinating critical mineral stockpiles among member nations and like-minded countries, aiming to reduce dependency on China for materials vital to defense and green energy industries.

headlines.headline: South Korea and Indonesia rein in derivatives trading to defend currencies amid energy shock
headlines.summary: Asian economies heavily reliant on imported energy are tightening oversight of speculative currency derivatives trades as the Iran war-driven oil disruption rattles forex markets.

headlines.headline: G7 leaders convene in France awaiting clarity on US-Iran agreement terms
headlines.summary: World leaders gathered at the G7 summit in Evian-les-Bains are seeking details on the US-Iran deal, with the Iran war and its implications dominating the agenda alongside trade disputes and the Ukraine conflict.

headlines.headline: Ebola risk for World Cup is extremely low, but US hospitals are ready, experts say
headlines.summary: US infectious disease experts say the risk of an Ebola case arriving during the 2026 World Cup is low but not zero, and American hospitals are prepared to respond if needed amid Congo's ongoing outbreak.

perspectives.angle: Ebola Risk for World Cup Extremely Low but US Hospitals Ready, Experts Say
perspectives.summary: US infectious disease experts say the risk of an Ebola case arriving during the 2026 World Cup is low but not zero, and American hospitals are prepared to respond if needed.

headlines.headline: China embraces Myanmar president as former junta chief seeks legitimacy
headlines.summary: Myanmar President Min Aung Hlaing's state visit to China gives the former junta chief a significant diplomatic boost as he seeks to consolidate power following a widely condemned election. Xi Jinping hosted the five-day visit in Beijing.

perspectives.angle: Myanmar’s Min Aung Hlaing makes first visit to China as civilian president
perspectives.summary: Myanmar’s former junta chief Min Aung Hlaing arrived in Beijing for a five-day visit at Xi Jinping’s invitation, his first trip to China since being elected president — a sign of deepening ties between the two authoritarian governments.

headlines.headline: Iran's US diaspora divided between protest and cheering at World Cup
headlines.summary: Iranian Americans are torn over Iran's participation in the 2026 World Cup, with some planning to watch the game and others protesting outside the LA stadium where the team played its opening match amid the backdrop of the US-Iran war.

headlines.headline: How the US-Israel war with Iran unfolded over 108 turbulent days
headlines.summary: A comprehensive timeline of the key moments in the 108-day US-Israel war with Iran that claimed thousands of lives and fundamentally reshaped the Middle East security landscape, from the first strikes to the tentative peace deal.

headlines.headline: G7 leaders to meet Zelensky as Trump signals possible Ukraine breakthrough
headlines.summary: G7 leaders will hold talks with President Zelensky on Tuesday as Trump suggested a possible breakthrough on Ukraine peace, shifting focus from the Iran deal to the ongoing Russia-Ukraine conflict at the summit in France.

headlines.headline: Social media overtakes traditional outlets as worlds leading news source for first time
headlines.summary: News consumers worldwide are increasingly turning to social media and video platforms for information, overtaking traditional outlets for the first time according to a Reuters Institute report, raising concerns about misinformation and media sustainability.

headlines.headline: Russia slashes fuel quality rules as Ukrainian attacks squeeze domestic supplies
headlines.summary: Russia is allowing refineries to produce lower-specification fuel for the domestic market as Ukrainian drone strikes on energy infrastructure create growing shortages, Kommersant reports.

perspectives.angle: Russia slashes fuel quality standards as Ukrainian drone strikes squeeze refineries
perspectives.summary: Russia is allowing refineries to produce fuel with lower environmental specifications for the domestic market, Kommersant reports, as Ukrainian drone strikes on Russian energy infrastructure have doubled since the start of 2026, forcing partial shutdowns and supply squeezes.

headlines.headline: Ebola cases top 800 in Congo as aid groups warn outbreak may be larger than reported
headlines.summary: The outbreak is already the third largest Ebola epidemic on record and the biggest caused by the Bundibugyo strain, with aid organizations warning the true scale may exceed official figures.

perspectives.angle: Ebola Outbreak Strains Health System in Eastern Congo as Cases Surge
perspectives.summary: A new Ebola outbreak in eastern Congo Ituri province is overwhelming an already fragile health system. Hospitals in Bunia are overwhelmed or shut down after suspected infections, while health workers race to contain the virus one month into the outbreak.

headlines.headline: IMF Chief Says No Global Slowdown Yet but Risks Remain High
headlines.summary: IMF Managing Director Kristalina Georgieva said the world economy is weathering the shock of the Middle East war with no signs yet of a global slowdown, but warned that risks remain elevated amid ongoing geopolitical uncertainties.

headlines.headline: Oil companies give Venezuela a second look amid Middle East chaos
headlines.summary: Trump's Middle East policy disruptions are creating an unexpected beneficiary: Venezuela. Oil companies experiencing FOMO are reconsidering Venezuelan opportunities as Mideast supply uncertainties persist.

headlines.headline: Senate Republicans demand a say on Trumps Iran deal
headlines.summary: Republican lawmakers said they are eager to examine the fine print of the US-Iran MOU, though there is a high bar to overturning any final agreement. The move signals congressional assertiveness on war powers.

headlines.headline: Zelensky Offers to Meet Putin at G7 or in the US for Peace Talks
headlines.summary: Ukrainian President Zelensky said he offered to meet Putin face-to-face at the G7 summit in France or in the United States, aiming to end the four-year war. Putin has not responded.

perspectives.angle: Zelensky Says Putin Rejected G7 Peace Talks Offer
perspectives.summary: After Moscow showed no willingness to meet around the G7, Kyiv is discussing a possible US-based format involving President Trump for Ukraine-Russia peace negotiations.

headlines.headline: Trump and Iran Dominate G7 Summit Opening Amid Hormuz Disputes
headlines.summary: The Iran deal overshadowed the G7 summit opening. Trump expressed confidence the Strait of Hormuz would reopen by Friday, but allies remained wary of tolls, mines, and enforcement gaps.

headlines.headline: White House Order to Pull Anthropic AI Models Fuels Warning of Ad Hoc Regulation
headlines.summary: The Trump administration forced Anthropic to disable its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models citing security concerns, drawing criticism that US AI regulation remains reactive and inconsistent rather than structured through clear policy.

headlines.headline: Commentary: Why the US Government Shut Down Anthropic's Latest Claude Model
headlines.summary: Channel News Asia analyzes how the US administration shifted from a hands-off AI posture to ordering Anthropic to disable its newest models, raising questions about sovereign AI development and the future of frontier model governance.

headlines.headline: Russia denies striking Kyiv monastery, claims Ukrainian Patriot missile caused damage
headlines.summary: Russia said it did not strike the historic Pechersk Lavra monastery in Kyiv during its overnight attack, instead blaming a U.S.-made Patriot air defence missile for the damage to the nearly 1,000-year-old site.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Scrambles for Air Defense Alternatives as Patriot Missiles Run Low
headlines.summary: Russia fired 34 ballistic missiles at Ukraine in a single overnight attack on Kyiv that set the historic Pechersk Lavra ablaze. With Patriot interceptors scarce and increasingly in demand globally, Kyiv is pursuing aging PAC-2/PAC-3 stockpiles and alternative systems to fill the gap.

headlines.headline: Zelensky Proposes Meeting Putin in the US After Russian Leader Ignores G7 Invitation
headlines.summary: Ukrainian President Zelensky said he offered to meet Putin in the United States during a call with Trump, suggesting Putin would find a US meeting difficult to refuse. The proposal came after the Russian leader reportedly ignored a G7 summit invitation.

headlines.headline: UK to Ban Social Media for Under-16s, May Impose Overnight Curfews
headlines.summary: The UK government announced it will ban social media for children under 16, requiring age verification and potentially imposing overnight curfews on platform access. Platforms including TikTok, Snapchat, and YouTube are in scope.

headlines.headline: Philippines’ Marcos and Russia’s Putin to Discuss Energy and Food Security
headlines.summary: Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. and Russian President Vladimir Putin are set to hold talks focused on energy cooperation and food security, a notable diplomatic engagement as Manila balances ties between Washington and Moscow.

headlines.headline: US and Iran sign framework deal with 60-day ceasefire, leaving major issues for future talks
headlines.summary: The United States and Iran signed an initial framework agreement that includes a 60-day ceasefire to pave the way for negotiations toward a final peace deal and discussions on Iran's nuclear program. Key details on the Strait of Hormuz reopening and sanctions relief remain unresolved.

perspectives.angle: US, Iran, Hezbollah spar over ceasefire terms as deal signing set for Friday in Switzerland
perspectives.summary: Iran insists Israel withdraw from southern Lebanon as part of the ceasefire deal, while the US Senate narrowly blocked a war powers resolution to end the Iran war. The official signing ceremony for the US-Iran deal is scheduled for Friday in Switzerland.

headlines.headline: Macron refuses to scrap France's digital tax, Trump threatens 100% wine tariffs at G7
headlines.summary: French President Macron said France will not bow to US pressure to drop its digital services tax on American tech giants, just hours before meeting Trump at the G7 summit. Trump reportedly threatened 100% tariffs on French wines if the tax isn't killed.

headlines.headline: Trump Says US Won't Need Much Help Reopening Hormuz
headlines.summary: President Trump told G7 allies the United States would not need "much help" in reopening the Strait of Hormuz, downplaying European offers of naval assistance for the strategic waterway.

headlines.headline: Hezbollah Says It Repelled Israeli Force Advancing in Southern Lebanon
headlines.summary: Hezbollah announced it repelled an Israeli force attempting to advance in southern Lebanon, raising fears of escalation along the Israel-Lebanon border despite the broader Iran ceasefire.

perspectives.angle: Hezbollah confronts Israeli troops advancing in southern Lebanon amid ongoing strikes
perspectives.summary: Hezbollah fighters fired rockets at Israeli troops near Majdal Zoun, forcing them to retreat, as Israel continued air strikes and issued evacuation warnings for 20 towns in southern Lebanon including Nabatieh.

headlines.headline: EU Parliament to Investigate Its Most Pro-Russian Member
headlines.summary: A special European Parliament committee is set to investigate Fernand Kartheiser, a Luxembourger MEP, for pro-Russian ties, highlighting internal EU tensions over Russia influence.

headlines.headline: Trump Threatens 100% Tariff on French Wines Over Digital Tax on Tech Firms
headlines.summary: President Trump threatened a 100% tariff on French wines in response to France imposing a digital services tax on US tech companies, escalating trade tensions at the G7 summit in France.

perspectives.angle: France 24: Trump Threatens 100% Tariff on French Wine and Champagne Over Digital Tax
perspectives.summary: President Trump warned the US will impose 100% tariffs on French wine and champagne unless Paris removes its digital services tax on American tech giants, escalating trade tensions on the eve of the G7 summit in France.

headlines.headline: UK bans under-16s from social media apps including TikTok and YouTube
headlines.summary: Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced a ban on social media for children under 16, covering apps like Snapchat, TikTok, and YouTube, with the restrictions expected to take effect in early 2027.

perspectives.angle: UK Bans Social Media for Under-16s and Romantic AI Chatbots for Under-18s
perspectives.summary: The UK government announced a ban on social media access for children under 16 and AI romantic chatbots for under-18s, calling it a step to give children back their childhood.

headlines.headline: France shuts down Israeli company stands at Eurosatory defense exhibition
headlines.summary: A dozen Israeli company stands were shut down at the Eurosatory defense show outside Paris, sparking outrage from Israeli authorities and raising tensions over arms exports during the Iran conflict.

perspectives.angle: Israeli Defense Firms Booths Boarded Up at Eurosatory After French Ban
perspectives.summary: France shut down Israeli exhibitor spaces at the Eurosatory defense trade show, boarding up booths of smaller Israeli firms while larger companies remain stripped of weapons displays, drawing sharp criticism from Israel’s Defense Ministry.

headlines.headline: France and Britain push multinational Hormuz naval mission as ceasefire nears
headlines.summary: France and Britain are advancing plans for a multinational naval mission to safeguard shipping through the Strait of Hormuz if a US-Iran ceasefire holds, though it remains unclear whether Tehran would accept any such foreign naval presence.

headlines.headline: Erdogan Says Turkey Chose Diplomacy Over Escalation in Iran Conflict
headlines.summary: President Erdogan stated Turkey pursued a measured, diplomacy-first policy during the US-Iran conflict, positioning Turkey as a regional mediator as the Iran deal takes shape.

headlines.headline: Israelis Across Political Spectrum Furious Over US-Iran Deal
headlines.summary: Israelis from across the political spectrum are expressing anger at the interim US-Iran peace deal, calling it a disaster and putting pressure on Netanyahu as Israel vows to keep forces in Lebanon and Syria.

perspectives.angle: Interim Iran Deal Sparks Fury Across Israeli Political Spectrum
perspectives.summary: Israelis from across the political spectrum reacted with anger to the US-Iran deal, with both hawks and doves calling it a strategic disaster for Israel and lashing out at Netanyahu.

headlines.headline: Somaliland Opens Embassy in Jerusalem After Israeli Recognition
headlines.summary: Somaliland opened its embassy in Jerusalem, months after Israel became the first country to recognize the breakaway African state, in a move with significant geopolitical implications for the Horn of Africa.

headlines.headline: Global Stocks Surge and Oil Prices Drop After US-Iran Deal
headlines.summary: Shares soared worldwide and oil prices fell after the US and Iran reached a tentative deal to end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, though analysts warn it could take weeks or months for oil flows to normalize.

headlines.headline: Ukraine officially launches EU membership negotiations amid ongoing war with Russia
headlines.summary: Ukraine began formal EU accession talks on Monday, starting a years-long process of reforms even as it continues fighting Russia's invasion. The move marks a historic shift for Kyiv's geopolitical orientation.

headlines.headline: EU Adopts New Russia Sanctions Targeting Energy Revenues and Propaganda
headlines.summary: The Council of the EU approved a new sanctions package against Russia, adding 34 individuals and 47 entities, with a focus on energy revenue flows and state propaganda infrastructure supporting the invasion of Ukraine.

headlines.headline: 4 Rescuers Killed in Russian Double-Tap Strike on Kharkiv
headlines.summary: Russian forces carried out a double-tap missile strike on Kharkiv, killing four rescue workers and injuring six others. The second missile hit approximately 30 seconds after the first, targeting emergency responders arriving at the scene.

headlines.headline: EU Sanctions Putins Confessor and Russias Prosecutor General in New Package
headlines.summary: The EU added 34 individuals and 47 organizations to its sanctions list, including Putin's personal confessor and Russia's Prosecutor General, expanding pressure on Moscow's inner circle and propaganda apparatus.

headlines.headline: London court convicts two men in Russian-linked plot to firebomb properties tied to UK Prime Minister
headlines.summary: Two men were convicted of an arson plot orchestrated by a mysterious Russian-speaking figure known as "El Money" who offered payment via Telegram to torch houses and a car linked to Keir Starmer.

headlines.headline: Drone strikes killed over 1,000 civilians in Sudan in first five months of 2026, UN rights chief says
headlines.summary: A senior UN official reported that drone strikes have killed more than 1,000 civilians in war-torn Sudan since January, highlighting the devastating toll of the ongoing civil conflict between the SAF and RSF.

headlines.headline: Macron Rebukes Trump: "The US Does Not Decide European Law" Over Digital Tax Dispute
headlines.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron pushed back forcefully against Trump's threat of 100% tariffs on French wine, declaring that Europe sets its own laws. The clash over France's 3% digital services tax on US tech firms threatens to overshadow the G7 summit in Évian.

headlines.headline: Al-Monitor: Turkish Foreign Minister Fidan to Visit Moscow for Ukraine, Black Sea Talks
headlines.summary: Turkey's top diplomat Hakan Fidan will travel to Moscow to reiterate Ankara's offer to host Russia-Ukraine peace negotiations, as the Black Sea security situation remains a critical concern for NATO.

headlines.headline: Trump critical minerals pricing plan faces skeptical G7 allies and divided industry
headlines.summary: The Trump administration push to form a Western critical minerals trading bloc and regulate prices faces skepticism from G7 partners and divisions within the US mining industry over costs and a Pentagon AI pricing system.

headlines.headline: Spain's Sanchez hails US-Iran deal as end to "senselessness" and new era for Middle East
headlines.summary: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez called the US-Iran agreement the end of "senselessness" and the start of a new era in the Middle East, urging the world to remember the costs of war and learn from the conflict.

headlines.headline: West Bank economy teetering toward collapse under Israeli restrictions, report finds
headlines.summary: A new report says Israel's web of restrictions is systematically dismantling the economy of the occupied West Bank, pushing it toward collapse and severely limiting economic opportunities for Palestinians.

headlines.headline: Israel strikes Hezbollah targets in Beirut suburbs after projectiles hit northern Israel
headlines.summary: Israel attacked Hezbollah targets in Beirut southern suburbs after the Iran-aligned group fired projectiles into Israeli territory. Lebanons state news agency reported two people killed in the strikes.

perspectives.angle: Israel strikes southern Beirut, killing at least 3; issues forced displacement orders for 29 Lebanese towns
perspectives.summary: Israeli strikes on Beirut killed at least three people as the military issued forced displacement orders for 29 towns in southern Lebanon, raising concerns the escalation could derail the nascent US-Iran peace deal.

headlines.headline: Iran Demands $12 Billion in Unfrozen Assets Before Formal Negotiations Begin
headlines.summary: Tehran has set a precondition for advancing beyond the framework deal: the US must release approximately $12 billion in frozen Iranian assets before formal negotiations proceed, adding a significant hurdle to the peace process.

headlines.headline: Indian Army Selects Israeli Meprolight MEPRO X6 as New Optic for Negev Machine Guns
headlines.summary: The Indian Army has chosen Meprolight's MEPRO X6 compact 6x telescopic day sight, with a range of up to 1,000 meters, as the new daytime optic for its Negev light machine guns, deepening India-Israel defense ties.

headlines.headline: African migrants with deep roots in South Africa flee xenophobic attacks in Durban
headlines.summary: Long-settled African migrants in Durban are fleeing xenophobic violence, with many having lived in South Africa since childhood. The displacement underscores rising anti-immigrant sentiment across the country.

perspectives.angle: WHO Chief Condemns South Africa Xenophobia as "Tragic Betrayal" as Migrants Flee Attacks
perspectives.summary: WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus denounced the latest wave of xenophobic violence in South Africa, calling it a tragic betrayal. African migrants with deep roots in the country are fleeing attacks amid rising anti-foreigner sentiment.

headlines.headline: Anti-G7 protesters torch Tesla and smash UN office windows in Geneva
headlines.summary: Geneva police fired teargas at protesters who set fire to a Tesla vehicle and smashed windows at a UN agency during violent demonstrations ahead of the G7 summit across the border in France.

headlines.headline: China AI stocks diverge as Zhipu soars while Minimax stumbles
headlines.summary: A widening valuation gap between China's two top LLM startups signals investor分化tion in the Chinese AI sector, with Zhipu gaining while Minimax loses ground.

headlines.headline: European aviation fuel supply tightens as Iran war cuts imports to 80%
headlines.summary: European aviation fuel imports remain at just 80% of pre-war levels despite US-Iran peace deal, with high prices and potential flight reductions expected in autumn.

headlines.headline: Singapore retrenchments rise sharply among degree holders and older workers
headlines.summary: Q1 2026 saw increased retrenchments in Singapore with the steepest increase among higher-educated workers, reflecting ongoing business restructuring in the city-state.

headlines.headline: Japan to resume domestic LNG carrier construction by 2035
headlines.summary: Three major Japanese shipbuilders plan to restart domestic LNG carrier construction by 2035, seeking technology cooperation from Korean shipyards after years without production.

headlines.headline: Taiwan Fires US-Supplied HIMARS Rockets Toward China in First
headlines.summary: Taiwan fired rockets from US-supplied HIMARS mobile launch systems toward China in a significant military exercise, showcasing its defensive capabilities amid escalating tensions with Beijing.

headlines.headline: North Korea Declares Nuclear Status Irreversible, Rejects Denuclearisation
headlines.summary: North Korea stated that its nuclear weapons status is now irreversible and that denuclearisation is 'terminated', marking a significant shift in its diplomatic stance.

headlines.headline: OPEC Lowers 2026 Global Oil Demand Growth Forecast
headlines.summary: OPEC cut its 2026 oil demand growth forecast for the second time, citing economic uncertainty and the impact of the Middle East conflict on global energy markets.

headlines.headline: UK Prime Minister Starmer Expected to Announce Social Media Ban for Young Teenagers
headlines.summary: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer is expected to announce a social media ban for young teenagers, with proposals reportedly going further than Australias similar restrictions. The move aims to protect childrens online wellbeing.

perspectives.angle: UK announces ban on social media for under-16s, following Australia model
perspectives.summary: Prime Minister Keir Starmer confirmed plans to ban social media access for children under 16, targeting platforms like TikTok, Snapchat, and YouTube, with rules expected to take effect in 2027.

headlines.headline: Putin Sends Birthday Greetings to Trump, Praises Him as Bright and Remarkable
headlines.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin extended birthday greetings to Donald Trump on his 80th birthday, describing him as a "bright, remarkable person and politician" and suggesting the two leaders could raise their countries ties to a new level.

headlines.headline: Russia suspected of using Swedish church near airport for espionage activities
headlines.summary: Swedish intelligence services suspect Moscow is using a Russian Orthodox church near Vasteras airport as a platform for espionage and hybrid activities, warning it could threaten national security.

headlines.headline: Only 25% of F-35 fleet fully mission capable as readiness rates decline
headlines.summary: A GAO report reveals the F-35's full mission capable rate fell to 25% through fiscal 2025, with readiness rates continuing to decline amid ongoing maintenance and sustainment challenges.

headlines.headline: Russia building major troop deployment infrastructure along NATO's Northern Flank
headlines.summary: Russia is constructing facilities for tens of thousands of troops near the Arctic, while NATO bolsters defenses with a new Arctic combat force as tensions rise on the alliance's northern border.

perspectives.angle: Russia builds major troop infrastructure along NATO's Arctic flank
perspectives.summary: Russia is constructing facilities for tens of thousands of troops near NATO's northern border, prompting the alliance to bolster its Arctic defenses with new combat capabilities.

headlines.headline: Germany and Spain launch 'Team Gen 6' after FCAS fighter program collapses
headlines.summary: Germany and Spain are forming a new coalition to develop sixth-generation fighter technology after the Franco-German FCAS program collapsed over irreconcilable differences on requirements.

perspectives.angle: Germany and France Scrap FCAS Fighter Jet Project
perspectives.summary: In a major blow to European defense cooperation, Germany and France have abandoned their ambitious FCAS fighter jet project after years of planning and billions in investment.

headlines.headline: Twelve Killed in Missouri Skydiving Plane Crash
headlines.summary: A plane carrying a pilot and 11 passengers on a skydiving outing crashed near Butler Memorial Airport in Missouri, killing all aboard. The aircraft caught fire shortly after takeoff around 11:30 AM local time.

headlines.headline: G7 Summit Begins in France as Anti-G7 Protests Erupt in Geneva
headlines.summary: World leaders convened for the G7 summit in France as thousands of protesters clashed with police in Geneva. Swiss authorities deployed water cannons and tear gas to disperse demonstrators ahead of the meeting.

headlines.headline: Swiss voters reject proposal to cap population at 10 million
headlines.summary: Nearly 55% of Swiss voters rejected a right-wing initiative to limit the population by cutting migration, in a referendum that framed the issue around affordability and sustainability.

perspectives.angle: Swiss voters reject right-wing bid to cap population at 10 million
perspectives.summary: Nearly 55% of Swiss voters rejected an initiative championed by the countrys top right-wing party to cap the Alpine nations population at 10 million, early results showed.

headlines.headline: Armenia releases final election results as pro-Russian opposition demands new vote
headlines.summary: Prime Minister Pashinyan's Civil Contract party won 64 of 105 parliamentary seats, but pro-Russian opposition groups are contesting the results and demanding new elections amid tensions over Armenia's pivot away from Moscow.

headlines.headline: Russian Black Sea Fleet command plans relocation from Crimea to Novorossiysk
headlines.summary: Pro-Ukrainian partisans claim Russian naval command is preparing to relocate its headquarters from occupied Crimea to the Russian mainland port of Novorossiysk, citing increasing Ukrainian strikes on the peninsula.

headlines.headline: Italian former general launches far-right party challenging Meloni government
headlines.summary: Roberto "Il Generale" Vannacci has founded a fast-rising far-right party that poses a threat to Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni's centre-right coalition and could reshape Italy's political landscape ahead of elections.

headlines.headline: US musician Oliver Tree among six killed in Brazil helicopter collision
headlines.summary: Two helicopters collided midair over Rio de Janeiro, killing all six people on board including the alternative singer and internet personality Oliver Tree, 32.

headlines.headline: China arrests US academic at conference for espionage activities
headlines.summary: Beijing authorities detained an American academic during a conference in China, accusing them of espionage, amid escalating US-China tensions.

perspectives.angle: China Arrests US Academic at Conference for Espionage
perspectives.summary: China detained Min Zin, a US academic specializing in Myanmar and Chinese foreign policy, during a conference on espionage charges. The arrest comes just a month after Trump visited Beijing amid already-strained US-China relations.

headlines.headline: British Royal Marines Seize Russian Shadow Fleet Oil Tanker
headlines.summary: UK forces intercepted an oil tanker in the English Channel suspected of being part of Russia sanctioned shadow fleet. Six-hour operation marks first time Britain acted alone to stop such a vessel.

perspectives.angle: UK unveils £210M Ukraine nuclear energy deal and sanctions on 600 Russian shadow fleet ships
perspectives.summary: Prime Minister Starmer announced enriched uranium supplies for Ukraine’s Energoatom and sweeping sanctions targeting Russia’s LNG shadow fleet at the G7 summit, aiming to choke Moscow’s war financing.

headlines.headline: Israel Expands Military Control by 1,000sq km Across Three Fronts
headlines.summary: Al Jazeera investigation reveals Israel has expanded military control by 1,000 square kilometers in Gaza, Lebanon, and Syria, using land grabs to mask strategic failures.

headlines.headline: UK and Japan Sign £18bn Investment Deal
headlines.summary: Japanese firms will invest billions in UK infrastructure and offshore wind projects. The agreement strengthens bilateral economic ties amid shifting global trade dynamics.
